Haiden (photographed July 2025)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Toi|Q3530413|WD=Q3530413|lt=Toi|1=Toi, Shizuoka}}729-1[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402055 Toi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office |位置 = {{coord|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}、former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |例祭 = Third Sat-Sun of October |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Toi Shrine'''（といじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Toi|Q3530413|WD=Q3530413|lt=Toi|1=Toi, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is the district's {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōchinju|1=UNKNOWN}} (general tutelary shrine). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to be very old, predating the Heian period, because it is regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate for "Toyomitama-no-Mikoto Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=List of shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Naka District of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}Precinct origin board. In {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745|1=1195}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 6), the Tominaga clan of Toi Castle (Takaya Castle) enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) from {{ill|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Q701403|WD=Q701403|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} as {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Hachimangū|Q11616249|WD=Q11616249|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū}}, and the shrine name was changed to "Toi Shrine." Since then it has retained strong character as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (the current deity includes {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253|1=1958}} (Shōwa 33), the shrine buildings were newly constructed. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） === Precincts === * Shrine buildings[http://kanko.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/form1.html?c1=5&pid=2521 Toi Shrine] - Izu City[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/723.html Toi Shrine] - Izu/Suruga tourism guide * Intergrown sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=zelkova|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} ("kuguri tree") * {{ill|lt=Komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} * Stone wall with kirikomi-hagi masonry * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} * Stone lanterns * Shrine office * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toi-zakura|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/136337.html Toi Shrine] - Shizuoka Shimbun * Heian great camphor tree * {{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} === Precinct shrines === * Banba Shrine（{{ill|lt=land deity|Q98760657|WD=Q98760657|1=land deity}}） * Toi Seirei Shrine（Gokoku Shrine） === Gallery === File:Toi-jinja 02.jpg|Torii File:Toi-jinja 01.jpg|Entrance File:Toi-jinja 03.jpg|Heian great camphor tree File:Toi-jinja 11.jpg|Precincts File:Toi-jinja 12.jpg|Intergrown tree File:Toi-jinja 05.jpg|Banba Shrine File:Toi-jinja 07.jpg|Toi Seirei Shrine === Cultural property === * Toi Shrine "Satsuma-korogashi"（city-designated folk cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail006921.html City-designated folk cultural properties] - Izu City）- a festival chant sung at Setsubun and the grand annual festival banquet. === Festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Setsubun festival|1=UNKNOWN}}（February 3）[http://www.sizcom.jp/area_news/20170331/914/ Toi Shrine Setsubun Festival] - Shizuoka Community Building Promotion Council * Grand annual festival（third Sat-Sun of October）[http://www.izutoi-shiosai.com/toi/matsuri/02_toi_hachiman_reitaisai.html Toi Hachiman Shrine Grand Festival] - Minshuku Shiosai ** {{ill|lt=Urayasu-no-mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} ** Toi taiko dedication ** Hamakudari ceremony (costumed procession) ** {{ill|lt=nagare-yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}}（{{ill|lt=Shuzenji Station|Q7505799|WD=Q7505799|1=Shuzenji Station}}-{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}} route）: 1 minute on foot northeast from "Banba" bus stop. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆市の建築物]]

In {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745|1=1195}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 6), the Tominaga clan of Toi Castle (Takaya Castle) enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) from {{ill|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Q701403|WD=Q701403|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} as {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Hachimangū|Q11616249|WD=Q11616249|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū}}, and the shrine name was changed to "Toi Shrine." Since then it has retained strong character as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (the current deity includes {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253|1=1958}} (Shōwa 33), the shrine buildings were newly constructed. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） === Precincts === * Shrine buildings[http://kanko.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/form1.html?c1=5&pid=2521 Toi Shrine] - Izu City[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/723.html Toi Shrine] - Izu/Suruga tourism guide * Intergrown sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=zelkova|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} ("kuguri tree") * {{ill|lt=Komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} * Stone wall with kirikomi-hagi masonry * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} * Stone lanterns * Shrine office * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toi-zakura|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/136337.html Toi Shrine] - Shizuoka Shimbun * Heian great camphor tree * {{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} === Precinct shrines === * Banba Shrine（{{ill|lt=land deity|Q98760657|WD=Q98760657|1=land deity}}） * Toi Seirei Shrine（Gokoku Shrine） === Gallery === File:Toi-jinja 02.jpg|Torii File:Toi-jinja 01.jpg|Entrance File:Toi-jinja 03.jpg|Heian great camphor tree File:Toi-jinja 11.jpg|Precincts File:Toi-jinja 12.jpg|Intergrown tree File:Toi-jinja 05.jpg|Banba Shrine File:Toi-jinja 07.jpg|Toi Seirei Shrine === Cultural property === * Toi Shrine "Satsuma-korogashi"（city-designated folk cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail006921.html City-designated folk cultural properties] - Izu City）- a festival chant sung at Setsubun and the grand annual festival banquet. === Festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Setsubun festival|1=UNKNOWN}}（February 3）[http://www.sizcom.jp/area_news/20170331/914/ Toi Shrine Setsubun Festival] - Shizuoka Community Building Promotion Council * Grand annual festival（third Sat-Sun of October）[http://www.izutoi-shiosai.com/toi/matsuri/02_toi_hachiman_reitaisai.html Toi Hachiman Shrine Grand Festival] - Minshuku Shiosai ** {{ill|lt=Urayasu-no-mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} ** Toi taiko dedication ** Hamakudari ceremony (costumed procession) ** {{ill|lt=nagare-yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}}（{{ill|lt=Shuzenji Station|Q7505799|WD=Q7505799|1=Shuzenji Station}}-{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}} route）: 1 minute on foot northeast from "Banba" bus stop. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izu, Shizuoka]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Toi|Q3530413|WD=Q3530413|lt=Toi|1=Toi, Shizuoka}}729-1[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402055 Toi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office |位置 = {{coord|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}、former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |例祭 = Third Sat-Sun of October |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Toi Shrine'''（といじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Toi|Q3530413|WD=Q3530413|lt=Toi|1=Toi, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is the district's {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōchinju|1=UNKNOWN}} (general tutelary shrine). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to be very old, predating the Heian period, because it is regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate for "Toyomitama-no-Mikoto Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=List of shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Naka District of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}Precinct origin board. In {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745|1=1195}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 6), the Tominaga clan of Toi Castle (Takaya Castle) enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) from {{ill|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Q701403|WD=Q701403|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} as {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Hachimangū|Q11616249|WD=Q11616249|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū}}, and the shrine name was changed to "Toi Shrine." Since then it has retained strong character as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (the current deity includes {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253|1=1958}} (Shōwa 33), the shrine buildings were newly constructed. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） === Precincts === * Shrine buildings[http://kanko.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/form1.html?c1=5&pid=2521 Toi Shrine] - Izu City[https://www.surugawan.net/guide/723.html Toi Shrine] - Izu/Suruga tourism guide * Intergrown sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=zelkova|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=camphor tree|1=UNKNOWN}} ("kuguri tree") * {{ill|lt=Komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} * Stone wall with kirikomi-hagi masonry * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} * Stone lanterns * Shrine office * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toi-zakura|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.at-s.com/facilities/article/view/temple/136337.html Toi Shrine] - Shizuoka Shimbun * Heian great camphor tree * {{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} === Precinct shrines === * Banba Shrine（{{ill|lt=land deity|Q98760657|WD=Q98760657|1=land deity}}） * Toi Seirei Shrine（Gokoku Shrine） === Gallery === File:Toi-jinja 02.jpg|Torii File:Toi-jinja 01.jpg|Entrance File:Toi-jinja 03.jpg|Heian great camphor tree File:Toi-jinja 11.jpg|Precincts File:Toi-jinja 12.jpg|Intergrown tree File:Toi-jinja 05.jpg|Banba Shrine File:Toi-jinja 07.jpg|Toi Seirei Shrine === Cultural property === * Toi Shrine "Satsuma-korogashi"（city-designated folk cultural property[http://www.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/gyousei_detail006921.html City-designated folk cultural properties] - Izu City）- a festival chant sung at Setsubun and the grand annual festival banquet. === Festivals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Setsubun festival|1=UNKNOWN}}（February 3）[http://www.sizcom.jp/area_news/20170331/914/ Toi Shrine Setsubun Festival] - Shizuoka Community Building Promotion Council * Grand annual festival（third Sat-Sun of October）[http://www.izutoi-shiosai.com/toi/matsuri/02_toi_hachiman_reitaisai.html Toi Hachiman Shrine Grand Festival] - Minshuku Shiosai ** {{ill|lt=Urayasu-no-mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} ** Toi taiko dedication ** Hamakudari ceremony (costumed procession) ** {{ill|lt=nagare-yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}}（{{ill|lt=Shuzenji Station|Q7505799|WD=Q7505799|1=Shuzenji Station}}-{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}} route）: 1 minute on foot northeast from "Banba" bus stop. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:伊豆市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izu, Shizuoka]]

Haiden (photographed July 2025)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Toi|Q3530413|WD=Q3530413|lt=Toi|1=Toi, Shizuoka}}729-1[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402055 Toi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office |位置 = {{coord|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}、former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |例祭 = Third Sat-Sun of October |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|54|39.6|N|138|47|54.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Toi Shrine'''（といじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Toi|Q3530413|WD=Q3530413|lt=Toi|1=Toi, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Izu|Q696840|WD=Q696840|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is the district's {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōchinju|1=UNKNOWN}} (general tutelary shrine). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to be very old, predating the Heian period, because it is regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate for "Toyomitama-no-Mikoto Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=List of shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Naka District of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}Precinct origin board. In {{ill|lt=1195|Q19745|WD=Q19745|1=1195}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 6), the Tominaga clan of Toi Castle (Takaya Castle) enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) from {{ill|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Q701403|WD=Q701403|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} as {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Hachimangū|Q11616249|WD=Q11616249|1=Wakamiya Hachiman‑gū}}, and the shrine name was changed to "Toi Shrine." Since then it has retained strong character as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (the current deity includes {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253|1=1958}} (Shōwa 33), the shrine buildings were newly constructed. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） === Precincts === * Shrine buildings[http://kanko.city.izu.shizuoka.jp/form1.html?c1=5&pid=2521 Toi Shrine] -
